race.name: Sovereign Lake Loppet
race.tagline: Fifty kilometers across the Okanagan Highland's snowbound plateau — where British Columbia's interior delivers cold, dry snow and cathedral forest silence.

climate.description: Late January on the Okanagan Highland at 1,600m delivers cold, dry, interior snow — the opposite of coastal BC's wet maritime conditions. Temperatures typically -15°C to -5°C. The plateau's elevation and continental position create some of Canada's most reliable and high-quality snow conditions for Nordic skiing.
challenges: Remote highland location — drive from Vernon or Kelowna
Occasional extreme cold snaps in the BC interior

course.primary: A 50km race on the Sovereign Lake Nordic Centre's extensive trail system at 1,600m on the Okanagan Highland. The course winds through dense subalpine forest with rolling terrain and technical sections. Sovereign Lake is regularly used for national-level competitions and offers some of the best grooming in western Canada.
course.surface: Machine-groomed trails at a dedicated Nordic centre; cold dry snow
course.grooming: Excellent grooming at a dedicated Nordic centre — one of the best in western Canada
features: Dedicated Nordic centre with 50+ km of trails
Cold, dry interior snow at 1,600m — excellent ski conditions
Subalpine forest setting in the Okanagan Highland
Both classic and skate options across multiple distances

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Strong grooming and snow reliability at a dedicated Nordic centre. The Okanagan Highland's cold, dry snow provides excellent conditions. National-level venue status reflects high organizational standards. Limited international draw and moderate field size keep it from higher tiers.

history.summary: The Sovereign Lake Loppet is held at the Sovereign Lake Nordic Centre near Vernon, British Columbia, one of western Canada's premier Nordic facilities. The centre sits at 1,600m on the Okanagan Highland, where cold interior air creates dry, fast snow conditions. The venue regularly hosts Canadian national championships and has developed a reputation for excellent grooming and reliable conditions.
notable_facts: Set at Sovereign Lake Nordic Centre at 1,600m on the Okanagan Highland
Regular host of Canadian national-level Nordic competitions
Cold, dry interior snow — among the best conditions in western Canada
Over 50km of groomed trails at the Nordic centre
Located in the Okanagan — better known for wine country in summer
